exercises.question: Mark the letter A, B, C or D to indicate the word whose underlined part differs from the other three in pronunciation: should, shout, about, amount
exercises.explanation: 'should' has /ʊ/ sound, while B, C, D have /aʊ/ diphthong.

exercises.question: Mark the letter A, B, C or D to indicate the word whose underlined part differs from the other three in pronunciation: shop, problem, top, glove
exercises.explanation: 'glove' has /ʌ/ sound, while A, B, C have /ɒ/ sound.

exercises.question: Mark the letter A, B, C or D to indicate the word that differs from the other three in the position of primary stress: rusty, career, accent, mission
exercises.explanation: 'career' has stress on the second syllable; A, C, D are stressed on the first.

exercises.question: Mark the letter A, B, C or D to indicate the word that differs from the other three in the position of primary stress: versatile, openness, financial, housekeeper
exercises.explanation: 'financial' has stress on the second syllable; A, B, D are stressed on the first.

exercises.question: She learned through bitter experience that he was not to be trust. (Find the underlined part that needs correction)
exercises.explanation: Should be 'trusted' (past participle) after 'to be' in passive voice.

exercises.question: Your classmates won't trust you if you're always told tales, Alvin. (Find the underlined part that needs correction)
exercises.explanation: Should be 'telling' tales (active, not passive). He tells tales, not is told tales.

exercises.question: About the guidance of the conductor, an orchestra creates music and harmonies. (Find the underlined part that needs correction)
exercises.explanation: Should be 'Under' the guidance of, not 'About'.

exercises.question: There are two points _____ I wanted to make.
exercises.explanation: 'which' is used as a relative pronoun for things (points).

exercises.question: Will we _____ to the wedding celebrations by them?
exercises.explanation: Passive voice (future): will + be + past participle.

exercises.question: I like my old phone, but this new model _____ it out of the water.
exercises.explanation: 'blows it out of the water' is an idiom meaning greatly surpasses.

exercises.question: If I were you, I more carefully in the rain.
exercises.explanation: Conditional type 2: If + past simple, would + V-infinitive.

exercises.question: All areas of _______ country will have _______ rain tonight.
exercises.explanation: 'the country' (specific) and 'some rain' (affirmative sentence).

exercises.question: She'll miss the bus if she _______ soon.
exercises.explanation: Conditional type 1: If + present simple. 'She' requires 'doesn't'.

exercises.question: You've done some stupid things before, but this really takes the _______.
exercises.explanation: 'takes the biscuit' is a British idiom meaning 'is the most remarkable/annoying'.

exercises.question: She left _______ an 'm' in accommodation.
exercises.explanation: 'left out' means omitted or forgot to include.

exercises.question: Is there _____ university _____ this town?
exercises.explanation: 'university' starts with /juː/ (consonant sound), so use 'a'. Location: 'in' a town.

exercises.question: John: "Can you do the cooking today?" Linda: "_______"
exercises.explanation: 'All right' is an agreement to do something when asked.

exercises.question: Linh: "Let's go to Nha Trang for the weekend!" Daniel: "_______"
options: A. Oh, you are good to say so
exercises.explanation: 'That's a good idea' is a natural way to agree to a suggestion.

exercises.question: It was inevitable that there would be job losses.
exercises.explanation: 'inevitable' means certain to happen, unavoidable.

exercises.question: A fund will be set up for the dead men's families.
exercises.explanation: 'set up' means established or created.

exercises.question: If petrol price goes up any more, I will have to use a bicycle.
exercises.explanation: The question asks for the OPPOSITE of 'goes up' (= increases). Options A (increase), B (ascend), D (raise) are all synonyms meaning to go higher. Only C ('develop') is not a synonym of 'goes up', making it the odd one out by elimination. Note: a true antonym would be 'decrease' or 'drop', but it is not among the choices.

exercises.question: How many countries took part in the last SEA Games?
exercises.explanation: 'took part in' means participated in.

exercises.passage: Rice is eaten by Vietnamese people every day. It often grows in tropical countries such as Vietnam, Thailand and Malaysia. People (23) _______ been growing rice for thousands of years. The seeds are planted in special beds to grow into young rice plants. Then they are (24) _______ to fields covered with muddy water called paddies. The fields of rice look very (25) _______. After 3 or 5 months, the rice is ready to be picked. People often drain away before collecting rice. Eating rice is (26) _______ special action in the world. They don't use spoons or forks to enjoy bowls of rice. However, they use two short sticks known (27) _______ chopsticks to put rice into their mouths.

questions.question: Fill blank (23)
questions.explanation: 'People' is plural, so 'have been growing' (present perfect continuous).

questions.question: Fill blank (24)
questions.explanation: Passive: 'are taken to fields'. Past participle after 'are'.

questions.question: Fill blank (26)
questions.explanation: 'a special action' - indefinite article before consonant sound.

exercises.passage: Discipline is an important virtue in one's life. Discipline means complete obedience to certain rules and regulations. Life without discipline is just like a house without a roof. It is absolutely essential for successful life. Discipline is the structural and fundamental unit of a successful person. It is essential for us at home, for soldiers in battlefield, for students in school, for players in playground. A team of experienced players often lose the match because of indiscipline in the team. A horrible battle can be won by a disciplined army. Discipline is very important in a student's life. We must obey our teachers, must follow the rules of the school. We should be sincere, dedicated, firm, and focus on our goals. If we violate, we will suffer a great deal in our future. As a student in the future of the country, we need to be very punctual to our routine, hard working, healthy and fit. Discipline demands self-control and dedication. It leads to the formation of a good society and nation as well.

questions.question: What is discipline?
options: A. obedience to rules and regulations
questions.explanation: The passage states: 'Discipline means complete obedience to certain rules and regulations.'

questions.question: What does the word "fundamental" mean?
questions.explanation: 'fundamental' means basic, essential, forming the foundation.

questions.question: Discipline is _______
questions.explanation: The passage states 'Discipline is very important in a student's life.'

questions.question: The word "follow" can best be replaced by _______
Discipline requires _______
questions.explanation: The passage states: 'Discipline demands self-control and dedication.'

exercises.original: "Why don't we go out for a walk?" Lan said
exercises.answer: Lan suggested going out for a walk.
acceptedAnswers: Lan suggested going out for a walk.
Lan suggested going out for a walk
Lan suggested that they should go out for a walk.
Lan suggested that they go out for a walk.
exercises.explanation: Chuyển câu đề nghị sang gián tiếp. 'Why don't we go out' → 'suggested going out for a walk'.

exercises.original: He started study Chinese five years ago.
exercises.answer: He has studied Chinese for five years.
acceptedAnswers: He has studied Chinese for five years.
He has studied Chinese for five years
He has been studying Chinese for five years.
He has been studying Chinese for five years
exercises.explanation: Chuyển quá khứ đơn + ago sang hiện tại hoàn thành + for. 'started five years ago' → 'has studied for five years'.

exercises.original: I think you should study harder for the exam.
exercises.answer: If I were you, I would study harder for the exam.
acceptedAnswers: If I were you, I would study harder for the exam.
If I were you, I would study harder for the exam
If I was you, I would study harder for the exam.
If I were you, I'd study harder for the exam.
exercises.explanation: Chuyển lời khuyên sang câu điều kiện loại 2. 'you should study harder' → 'If I were you, I would study harder'.

exercises.original: Your dictionary is more expensive than mine.
exercises.answer: My dictionary is not as expensive as yours.
acceptedAnswers: My dictionary is not as expensive as yours.
My dictionary is not as expensive as yours
My dictionary isn't as expensive as yours.
My dictionary is cheaper than yours.
exercises.explanation: Chuyển so sánh hơn sang so sánh bằng (phủ định). 'more expensive than mine' → 'not as expensive as yours'.

exercises.original: It's a pity. I can't play the guitar.
exercises.answer: I wish I could play the guitar.
acceptedAnswers: I wish I could play the guitar.
I wish I could play the guitar
exercises.explanation: Wish + past simple/could to express regret about present inability.

exercises.original: I don't really like her. I admire her achievements.
exercises.answer: Although I don't really like her, I admire her achievements.
acceptedAnswers: Although I don't really like her, I admire her achievements.
Although I don't really like her, I admire her achievements
I admire her achievements although I don't really like her.
I admire her achievements although I don't really like her
exercises.explanation: 'although' introduces a concessive clause showing contrast.

exercises.original: She has written many essays. This is the best one.
exercises.answer: This is the best essay she has ever written.
acceptedAnswers: This is the best essay she has ever written.
This is the best essay she has ever written
This is the best essay that she has ever written.
exercises.explanation: Superlative + present perfect with 'ever': the best... ever + V-ed.

exercises.original: It's raining heavily. We cancelled our journey.
exercises.answer: It's raining heavily, so we cancelled our journey.
acceptedAnswers: It's raining heavily, so we cancelled our journey.
It's raining heavily, so we cancelled our journey
It was raining heavily, so we cancelled our journey.
It was raining heavily, so we cancelled our journey
exercises.explanation: 'so' connects cause and result.
